text: Is there a way for subagents to ask for review of files and somehow plug in auto-reads so that the generalist subagent doesn't waste time like doing tool actions for discoverability or at least it minimizes them and can get to answers faster.

we need some kind of cache observability nd stability monitoring that outputs log for the agent to notify the user when there were uses that should be resolved with the harness are werent merely things like waiting too long to respond so it went cold
subagent need ability to cascade available models defined in them in tier
Silent runner checks should not trigger the attention notification when failed because agent is not complete yet

I have this idea that only traceable code paths should be tested instead of needing to always test all unit tests. Like if there's a way to trace a code path from a test, and if nothing about that code path has changed, the test should not re-execute almost like a test cache.
subagents should auto compact after they complete in case they get called again to save tokens and money
Context sync should only be run when the agent feels it's actually done with its work before it tries to end the turn.
The context sync tool call should show some kind of progress with what's happening.

We need to better defend against delete and add changes in patch because I believe they fail and you're meant to be using replace or literally just using add to overwrite and so I wonder if A we can treat a delete and then add in a way where it it it analyzes all the files being changed and it sees if there is an add over a delete and then it just does essentially the operation of a replace else the prompting needs stronger guidance.
session naming should not happen for subagents
a way to fire off small command like run cli and give answer. like a fire forget system thats outside of context

way to connect a editor session in something like helix to pi to send over highlighted lines as a line range with a comment to give the agent exact feedback on what you dont like. Maybe it should include the line itself too, not sure yet
a way to trim old sessions easily
focus feature and goal setting
i want a deterministic compaction that literally jsut wipes things out and keeps last few user messages and assistant messages and a small log of tools that ran or a no tools version

What about something like a smart compaction? If we keep with something like postures, what about after planning, if the agent wants to switch over to acting, it needs to have a plan file and it should essentially mark files to be re-read and it should completely purge context history and start with in the way compaction does but like it's automatic it doesn't send to an LM just basically does the same thing compaction does but without putting in the summary basically and well it auto-generates a summary based on like this whole concept of the forget mechanism but it's like essentially it needs to mark things as keep, whatever would be better, forget or keep.

It could do both, either or whichever it thinks is more efficient in the particular use case and based on that it keeps those exact context items like reads that it should keep so it doesn't have to read them again through another tool call, but it gets rid of everything that it shouldn't have anymore.
update tau-new to allow you to support tagging reference examples and inform the agent to make decisions based on its context. Should also auto inject docs based on what you are creating.
we need whole file structural maps when agent tries to read whole file
context injection autoread should seed Tau read cache
ability to disable certain subagents
auto compact subagents after they complete a run so if invoked again its not running up the bill
allow agent to manage its own context window with context reminder and self compaction infra
